metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes; daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:exit_clean+sigkill+restart — process exit deletes the session by design, but the kiro-cli driver stubs the teardown primitives the 3-variant recipe needs.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: gap:exit_clean (also gap:sigkill, gap:restart)

The session-end scenario has three variants — (1) clean exit, (2) SIGKILL while idle in `ready`, (3) SIGKILL mid-turn while `working`. kiro-cli 2.5.1 supports all three teardown paths as-shipped, the daemon observes the disappearance by design, and the only thing missing is driver tooling: the three teardown step types the recipe needs are stubbed in the kiro-cli interactive driver.

## agent_supports: yes

All three teardowns are native, no special feature required:

- **Variant 1 (clean exit).** `/quit` (alias `/exit`) ends the chat cleanly: kiro prints "Session ended.", removes the `<uuid>.lock` live-session marker, and prints "Resume with: kiro-cli --resume-id <uuid>" (FINDINGS.md §8). The `kiro-cli` parent process exits.
- **Variants 2 & 3 (SIGKILL).** `kill -9` on the `kiro-cli` parent PID terminates the process tree regardless of turn state — this is OS process death, not a CLI feature. The process tree is `kiro-cli` (parent, comm=`kiro-cli`) → `kiro-cli-chat` → `bun tui.js` + `acp` (FINDINGS.md §1); killing the parent collapses the session.

## daemon_capability: full

This is the load-bearing pillar and it is verified live, not just read off the parser.

**Process-exit → session removed BY DESIGN.** The kiro-cli adapter matches on `ExactName{Name: "kiro-cli"}` and discovers the PID by cwd (`DiscoverPIDByCWD`, agent.go / pid.go). `pgrep -x kiro-cli` matches ONLY the parent and never collides with the always-running `kiro_cli_desktop` companion (FINDINGS.md §1). When the parent dies — clean `/quit` OR `kill -9` — the process scanner stops matching it, emits `process_exited`, and the standard ready-TTL removes the session row. This was verified LIVE: `/quit` removed the session from the dashboard.

**The `ready → working → ready` arc per variant** is produced by the parser (parser.go, FINDINGS.md §5): a `Prompt` event → `user_message` drives `ready → working`; the FINAL text-only `AssistantMessage` of a turn → `turn_done` drives `working → ready`; mid-turn `AssistantMessage`s carry `toolUse` blocks and keep the session `working`. The transcript live-appends (FINDINGS.md §2), and the session shows `working` mid-turn, `ready` after the text-only assistant message — exactly the arc the scenario asserts.

**Variant 3 (SIGKILL mid-turn).** The session is `working` when the kill fires; the process disappears with no turn-end event. The daemon's process-exit sweep emits `process_exited` and resolves the dangling `working` (the scenario's "state does not stick in `working`"). As with the claudecode cell, whether the sweep's working→ready transition lands inside the recording's tail window is timing-dependent — the live dashboard always settles correctly because the sweep keeps running; the spec accommodates this by anchoring v3_exit to v3_turn_start with a wide budget rather than requiring an explicit post-crash ready phase.

Every user-observable signal the scenario asserts (state arc, disappearance within readyTTL, no stick-in-working) is emitted by the adapter as it exists today → `full`, not `bug` or `incapable`.

## driver_capability: gap:exit_clean (+ gap:sigkill, gap:restart)

The 3-variant recipe needs three teardown step types. The kiro-cli interactive driver elicits ONLY `send slash wait_turn sleep` (its `DRIVE_ELICITS` constant, driver-interactive.sh) — every teardown arm is present but stubbed as `not_implemented`:

- **`exit_clean`** — variant 1's clean `/quit`. (A `/quit` IS a `slash` send mechanically, but the recording needs a teardown primitive that quits the TUI and resolves the staged transcript path BEFORE process exit; the dedicated `exit_clean` step — Ctrl-D/`/quit` + drain — is the right primitive, mirroring claudecode's recipe.)
- **`sigkill`** — variants 2 & 3's `kill -9` of the active session PID.
- **`restart`** — to start a FRESH session (new UUID, new cwd) between the three variants, so the recording shows three distinct session rows separated by grey gaps.

This is tooling work, not an observability limit: the cell keeps a real recipe + spec, and `record` ports `exit_clean` / `sigkill` / `restart` from drive-claudecode-interactive.sh before it drives. Naming the primary blocker as `gap:exit_clean` (the first teardown the recipe hits); `sigkill` and `restart` are listed in caveats so `record` ports all three.

## Confidence

0.85 — the daemon pillar is anchored in a LIVE observation (`/quit` removed the session) plus the committed adapter source, and the agent pillar is direct OS process behavior. The provisional edge is variant-3's sweep-to-ready timing within the recording tail (timing-dependent, same caveat as claudecode), which `record` will settle.
caveats: Driver gap is plural: variant 1 needs `exit_clean`, variants 2 & 3 need `sigkill`, and the inter-variant fresh sessions need `restart`. All three arms are present-but-stubbed (`not_implemented`) in drive-kiro-cli-interactive.sh; `record` must port all three from drive-claudecode-interactive.sh, not just `exit_clean`.

Variant 3 (SIGKILL mid-turn): the post-crash sweep-to-ready transition may not land inside the recording's tail window — the live dashboard settles via the sweep, but the recording can miss it. The spec accommodates this by anchoring v3_exit to v3_turn_start rather than requiring an explicit post-crash ready phase (same shape as the claudecode session-end cell).

kiro-cli's JSONL carries no model/token/cost fields (FINDINGS.md §4); those live in the <uuid>.json sidecar which the parser does not read in v1. So observations assert model 'unknown' and cost/tokens zero — orthogonal to this scenario, which asserts lifecycle only.

`restart` for kiro-cli does NOT need a fresh cwd per session the way claudecode does (claude caches per-directory trust dialogs); kiro-cli launches with `--trust-all-tools` (persisted consent), so the trust picker never stalls. A fresh cwd is still cleanest for an unambiguous cwd-based PID match across the three variants.

sources.note: §8 /quit prints 'Session ended.' + removes <uuid>.lock + 'Resume with…'; §1 process tree (parent comm=kiro-cli, no kiro_cli_desktop collision); §2 live-append; §5 turn-end shape (text-only AssistantMessage = turn_done). LIVE-verified: /quit removed the session from the dashboard (process exit → session deleted by design).

Match: ExactName{Name: "kiro-cli"}; PIDForSession: DiscoverPID. When pgrep -x kiro-cli stops matching the dead parent, the scanner emits process_exited and the ready-TTL removes the row — the disappearance the scenario asserts.

Prompt→user_message (ready→working); text-only AssistantMessage→turn_done (working→ready); mid-turn AssistantMessage carries toolUse and stays working — produces the per-variant ready→working→ready arc.
DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep" only; exit_clean / sigkill / restart arms are present but stubbed not_implemented → driver=gap. record ports them from drive-claudecode-interactive.sh.
Reference 3-variant recipe (exit_clean / restart / sigkill) and the variant-3 tail-window caveat this cell mirrors.

preconditions: kiro-cli on PATH and authenticated (subscription auth; launch with `kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools` so the per-tool picker never stalls — FINDINGS.md §8)
irrlichd is recording (run-cell isolated mode or --attach against a daemon started with --record + grant-all)
no other kiro-cli session is alive in any of the recipe's cwds (cwd-based PID match)
setup: Driver launches a fresh `kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools` per variant under <staging>/cwd, cwd-2, cwd-3; kiro mints its own <uuid>.jsonl per launch (discovered as the newest session file with mtime > MARKER).

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: ok
Write a 200 word essay about the history of coffee.

verify: events.jsonl contains 3 distinct UUID session_ids (one per variant) plus their proc-<PID> pre-session rows
events.jsonl contains 3 process_exited events, one per UUID
variant 1: the first UUID session returns to ready right after its turn completes, immediately preceding its process_exited (proves 'State is ready at exit')
variant 2: the second UUID session ends in ready (idle) when sigkill fires; no state_transition to working appears after process_exited
variant 3: the third UUID session is in working when sigkill fires; the sweep-promotes-to-ready transition may or may not appear before transcript_removed within the 8s tail — accept either shape
the viewer state band shows three colored arcs separated by grey gaps where no session is alive between variants
